title: Vast signs additional partners for commercial space station microgravity research
summary: Vast has expanded its network of partners for microgravity research and manufacturing on its commercial space station. The company announced the addition of several new collaborators on June 24.

title: York satellite demonstrates two-way UHF communications from low Earth orbit
summary: York Space Systems announced that a satellite built for the U.S. Space Force successfully demonstrated two-way tactical communications using UHF links from low Earth orbit. This achievement marks a significant milestone in tactical satellite communications.

title: How space weather could bust the AI boom
summary: The rapid expansion of AI data centers is straining America's electric grid, and space weather events could exacerbate this challenge by disrupting power infrastructure. This highlights the vulnerability of critical systems to solar activity amid growing technological demands.

title: Vantor selects BAE Systems to build next-generation imaging satellites
summary: Vantor has selected BAE Systems to develop its next-generation high-resolution imaging satellites. This partnership reunites Vantor with the former Ball Aerospace team, which previously contributed to DigitalGlobe's satellite programs.

title: SpaceX launches secretive Starfall reentry demo mission
summary: SpaceX conducted the first test flight of its secretive Starfall reentry capsule on June 23. The mission's details remained largely undisclosed, maintaining the program's secrecy.

title: NGSO trade association launches without industry giant SpaceX
summary: A new trade association has been launched by Amazon and other non-geostationary satellite operators to represent their growing market, excluding SpaceX despite its dominant NGSO constellation.

title: OHB raises funding for expansion, acquisitions
summary: OHB, a German space company, plans to raise approximately half a billion euros through a stock sale to expand its facilities and pursue acquisitions. The funding will support the company's growth and strategic expansion in the space industry.

title: NASA’s inspector general warns launch sites nearing capacity
summary: NASA's inspector general report highlights that launch sites are nearing capacity due to the increasing number of launches. This issue is becoming a growing concern for spaceports.

title: Boeing wins $2 billion Space Force contract for communications satellites
summary: Boeing secured a $2 billion contract to construct two advanced military communications satellites for the U.S. Space Force, defeating Lockheed Martin in a competitive bid.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes New Associate Member
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of Sophia Space as a new associate member. Sophia Space is focused on developing orbital computing infrastructure for advanced space-based data processing.

title: NASA looks for the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS | Space photo of the day for June 24, 2026
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is analyzing the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS to determine its origins. The findings could provide insights into the early solar system and interstellar objects.

title: ESA chief calls for greater European space autonomy as trust in US partnership erodes
summary: ESA's director general advocates for increased European space autonomy due to eroding trust in U.S. partnerships, highlighted by the cancellation of key projects like Gateway and Mars Sample Return.

title: It's a crime these Lego Star Wars sets are being retired but you can save big before they go
summary: The news discusses the retirement of Lego Star Wars sets and mentions significant discounts on these discontinued models ahead of the Star Wars 50th anniversary. The author expresses disappointment over the retirement of the sets.

title: 'Everybody wanted to be the first': Apollo astronauts were more competitive, Artemis 2 pilot says
summary: NASA's Artemis moon program astronauts are less competitive compared to Apollo-era astronauts, according to the Artemis 2 pilot. The Artemis program is designed with a different approach to astronaut attitudes and collaboration.

title: Hubble Space Telescope images galaxy scientists thought was impossible to find
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope captured images of a distant galaxy that scientists believed was impossible to find, as it appears to have cleared the cosmic hydrogen fog from the early universe.

title: What is Starfall? A look at SpaceX's mysterious new return capsule
summary: SpaceX launched its Starfall return capsule for the first time. The company aims to use the new vehicle for undisclosed purposes.

title: Space.com just launched an app! Get your inside scoop on space exploration
summary: Space.com has launched a new app providing daily updates on space exploration, including mission news, discoveries, and night sky guides.

title: These anti-Prime deals are giving Amazon a good run for their money
summary: Anti-Prime Day deals are offering discounts on skywatching gear from retailers like Walmart, Adorama, and BH Photo & Video, challenging Amazon's dominance in the market.

title: Rocket Lab launches US Space Force mission with less than 17 hours' notice — a new record
summary: Rocket Lab launched the second satellite for the U.S. Space Force's Victus Haze mission with less than 17 hours' notice, setting a new launch readiness record. The mission involves two spacecraft preparing to rendezvous and perform dynamic engagements.

title: This might be your last chance to pick up a retired Lego Star Wars set before it flies off forever, and it's under $100 on Amazon for Prime Day
summary: A retired Lego Star Wars Coruscant Guard Gunship set is currently under $100 on Amazon during Prime Day, offering a rare chance to purchase it before it becomes unavailable.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4927–4933: Let’s Drive to That Smooth Area
summary: Curiosity's science team is planning to drive the rover to a smoother area based on orbital images showing varied surface textures. The blog post is authored by a planetary mineralogy professor at The Open University, UK.

title: Playing the Moon Game
summary: Apollo astronauts rehearsed their lunar field geology roles in Alaska's Valley of Ten Thousand Smokes. The training simulated conditions they would face on the Moon.

title: NASA Names Sean Gallagher as Chief Information Officer
summary: NASA has appointed Sean Gallagher as its Chief Information Officer (CIO), responsible for the agency's IT portfolio. Gallagher has been serving in an acting role since January and now assumes the position permanently.

title: CSDA Selects Eight Commercial Satellite Data Providers for On-Ramp 2 Contract Awards
summary: NASA's Commercial Smallsat Data Acquisition (CSDA) program selected eight commercial satellite data providers for its On-Ramp 2 contract awards. These providers will supply data to support NASA's Earth science research and priorities.

title: 58th Girl Scouts Unite Event
summary: NASA will participate in the 58th Girl Scouts Unite Event with Hyperwall storytelling sessions led by experts like Barbara Hilton and Lindsay Hays. The event takes place July 23-25, 2026, at the Exhibit Hall (Booth #206).

title: 2026 ALA Hyperwall Schedule
summary: NASA will participate in the 2026 ALA Annual Conference with Hyperwall storytelling sessions at Booth #2243 from June 25-29, 2026. The event will feature presentations by NASA experts on the Hyperwall agenda.

title: This is How NASA Flight Tests New Technology
summary: NASA emphasizes the importance of flight tests in transforming innovative ideas into practical technologies. These tests have historically shaped advancements like supersonic human flight and the space shuttle design.

title: Hanging in the Balance
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a striking image of the Moon on flight day 6, showcasing its half-illuminated surface and rugged terrain. The photo highlights the stark contrast between light and dark regions, offering a unique perspective of the lunar landscape.

title: ARMD Research Solicitations (Updated June 23)
summary: NASA's Aeronautics Research Mission Directorate (ARMD) updated its solicitations page on June 23, 2026, inviting collaboration on aeronautical research to improve air transportation systems. The page provides opportunities to work with NASA's innovators through their research programs.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week will see six orbital launches globally, including the first flight of Pegasus XL. The launches will also feature Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This marks another busy month of operations aboard the station.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space's Terran R second stage has been transported to Stennis Space Center as the company progresses toward its planned 2026 debut of the heavy-lift vehicle. This marks a key milestone in the development of the fully reusable rocket.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. This follows the recent completion of Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after a previous failure.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a significant advancement in robotic arm technology for space applications. It highlights a breakthrough that could revolutionize how robotic arms operate in space.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure ESA's spacecraft can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these researchers to support mission success.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association to represent non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This new trade association aims to advocate for the interests of NGSO operators in the evolving space industry.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the emerging reentry sector. This event validates new startups entering the reentry technology space.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. A report from NASA's inspector general highlights this impending infrastructure challenge.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit. The initiatives aim to advance the company's orbital-compute capabilities.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered satellite platform for maritime security. The funding aims to accelerate commercialization of the technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are revolutionizing space access by offering a faster and more economical alternative to traditional methods. Companies can now leverage existing spacecraft and infrastructure instead of building everything from scratch.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, enabling more data downlinks across a wider range of frequencies. This development supports the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40M Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to $63.5M.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping during ground tests of its quantum networking satellite, Q4S. The test marks a milestone ahead of its planned 2027 flight.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 with its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, achieving all test objectives and being fully commissioned.

title: Northwood Space Debuts Parabolic Antenna, Designed for High-Volume Data
summary: Northwood Space introduced the Prism parabolic antenna, a 2.4-meter ground system designed to support high-volume data connectivity. The new technology aims to enhance communication capabilities for space applications.

title: Space Sector Faces 400% Surge in Cyberattacks Amid Iran War, Experts Say
summary: The space sector has experienced a 400% increase in cyberattacks following U.S. and Israel-led military operations in Iran. Cybersecurity experts highlight the rising tempo and sophistication of these attacks.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Selects Apex Space Bus for Orbital Compute Demo
summary: Sophia Space has raised $7 million in funding and selected Apex Space's Nova satellite bus for an orbital computing demonstration mission planned for 2027.

title: SpaceX Launches New Microgravity Lab Demo, Starfall
summary: SpaceX demonstrated a new reentry vehicle called Starfall, aimed at enabling regular access to microgravity for science and in-space manufacturing. The spacecraft was launched for the first time on Tuesday.

title: Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the responsive space mission Victus Haze for the U.S. Space Force from New Zealand. The mission is part of the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) program.

title: Building a Global Framework for D2D With Skylo’s Mindel De La Torre
summary: Skylo's Mindel De La Torre discusses the rapid evolution of direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity, which has transitioned from a technological milestone to widespread deployment in millions of phones.

title: EchoStar Swaps SATS Ticker for ECHO to Emphasize Business Shift
summary: EchoStar is changing its stock ticker from 'SATS' to 'ECHO' to reflect its transition into a global connectivity business. The change took effect on June 24.

title: Jio Evaluates a Sovereign LEO Constellation for India
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom operator, is considering launching a sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) constellation for satellite broadband. The company is exploring a 'dual approach' to enhance connectivity.

title: MDA Space to Acquire Blue Canyon from Raytheon to Reach US Defense Market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring spacecraft manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies from Raytheon to strengthen its position in the U.S. defense market. The acquisition aims to expand MDA Space's capabilities and market reach.

title: Former SES Exec John-Paul Hemingway to Lead Vast’s Satellite Business
summary: Former SES executive John-Paul Hemingway has joined Vast as senior vice president of satellite business development and commercialization. Hemingway will lead Vast's new satellite manufacturing business.
